title: 13 Best Herbal Teas For Gum Swelling
intro: Herbal teas for gum swelling are a natural remedy used to treat inflammation and pain associated with swollen gums, also known as gingivitis or periodontitis. These herbal teas can effectively treat this ailment due to their anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce swelling, fight off infections, and soothe irritated tissues. Examples of herbal teas that can be used to treat gum swelling include peppermint tea, which has antibacterial properties that help to fight off infections, chamomile tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling, and sage tea, which has antioxidant properties that help to soothe irritated tissues. Other herbal teas that can be used to treat gum swelling include eucalyptus tea, which helps to reduce inflammation and fight off infections, ginger tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and swelling, and licorice root tea, which has antibacterial properties that help to fight off infections.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat gum swelling ailments, such as gingivitis and periodontitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents present in this herbal preparation, including alkylamides, caffeic acid, and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections. By reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth, Echinacea purpurea teas can help to alleviate symptoms of gum swelling and promote overall oral health. Regular consumption of Echinacea purpurea teas may also boost the immune system, which can aid in the body's natural defense against gum infections and promote faster recovery.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea roots and flowers.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the Echinacea mixture in a cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into another cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, for a sweeter taste.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for gum swelling relief.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat gum swelling, but potential side effects may include allergic reactions, headaches, and stomach upset in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive digestive systems or allergies. To avoid any adverse reactions, it is recommended to start with small doses, monitor your body's response, and avoid using the tea if you experience any discomfort, and also be cautious when consuming it with other medications or certain health conditions.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea 3 times a day as needed.
Start with 1 tablespoon of dried root per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes and then strain the tea.
Use for 7-10 days to help reduce gum swelling and inflammation.
Consult a healthcare professional for guidance and further treatment.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been used traditionally to treat gum swelling ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, including gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. By consuming ginger tea, individuals can benefit from its ability to reduce swelling and promote healing, ultimately leading to a faster recovery from gum-related issues. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger tea make it an effective herbal preparation for treating gum swelling ailments, providing relief and comfort to those affected.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of fresh ginger root and a cup of water.
Peel the ginger root and chop it into small pieces.
Boil the cup of water in a pot and add the chopped ginger pieces.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink the ginger tea warm or at room temperature, 2-3 times a day for gum swelling.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can help alleviate gum swelling, but possible side effects may include heartburn, nausea, and diarrhea due to the high ginger content, which can irritate the digestive system. To minimize potential side effects, individuals using Zingiber officinale teas to treat gum swelling should take the tea in moderation, ideally in small amounts with food and plenty of water, to help alleviate digestive discomfort.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor for proper diagnosis and treatment plan for gum swelling.
Drink 1-2 cups of Zingiber officinale tea 3 times a day for 7-10 days.
Use 2-3 teaspoons of dried Zingiber officinale root per cup of boiling water.
Maintain a healthy oral hygiene routine, including regular brushing and flossing.
Monitor gum swelling and adjust treatment plan as advised by the doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas, derived from the leaves of the sage plant, have been traditionally used to treat gum swelling ailments such as gingivitis and periodontitis. The anti-inflammatory properties of Salvia officinalis teas help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Salvia officinalis teas, including rosmarinic acid, caffeic acid, and ursolic acid, contribute to its therapeutic effects by exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which help to combat bacterial infections and prevent tissue damage. By incorporating Salvia officinalis teas into one's oral care routine, individuals can benefit from its ability to soothe and protect the gums, promoting overall oral health and preventing the progression of gum disease.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Rinse the leaves with cold water and remove any stems or debris.
Place the leaves in the tea infuser or strainer and pour the boiling water over them.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into a cup.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help reduce gum swelling.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be used to treat gum swelling, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, allergic reactions, or interact with other medications, especially those for diabetes or blood thinning. Precautions when using Salvia officinalis teas for gum swelling include avoiding excessive consumption, monitoring blood sugar levels, and being aware of potential allergic reactions, especially if you have a known allergy to plants in the Lamiaceae family.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Salvia officinalis tea 3 times a day.
Take 1 teaspoon of dried Salvia officinalis leaves per cup of boiling water.
Soak a cotton ball in the tea and apply it to the swollen gum.
Leave the cotton ball on the gum for 15-20 minutes, 3 times a day.
Continue for 5-7 days, or until the gum swelling reduces significantly.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used to treat gum swelling ailments, such as gingivitis and periodontitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, in Eucalyptus globulus teas help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial growth, thereby alleviating gum swelling. By consuming Eucalyptus globulus teas, individuals can experience the benefits of reduced gum inflammation, improved oral health, and a lower risk of gum disease progression. The antimicrobial properties of Eucalyptus globulus teas also help to prevent the spread of infection and promote a healthy oral environment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh eucalyptus leaves (Eucalyptus globulus) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Chop the eucalyptus leaves into small pieces.
Steep the chopped eucalyptus leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the eucalyptus leaves.
Drink 1/2 cup of the eucalyptus tea 3-4 times a day for gum swelling relief.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can help alleviate gum swelling by reducing inflammation and promoting healing, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, such as skin rashes or itching, and gastrointestinal issues like nausea or diarrhea. To minimize these risks, it is recommended to start with small doses and monitor your body's response, avoid consuming Eucalyptus globulus teas in excess, and be cautious if you have any underlying health conditions, especially allergies or digestive issues.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of warm Eucalyptus globulus tea 3 times a day.
Swish 10ml of tea in your mouth for 30 seconds, then spit it out.
Repeat step 2 for 2-3 minutes, 3 times a day.
Use Eucalyptus globulus tea consistently for 7-10 days.
Monitor gum swelling and consult a doctor if it persists.

preparations.herb_desc: Mentha x piperita teas, commonly known as peppermint tea, have been traditionally used to treat gum swelling or gingivitis due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Mentha x piperita, including menthol and menthone, help to reduce inflammation and fight off bacterial infections that can cause gum swelling. The antimicrobial properties of peppermint tea help to prevent the growth of bacteria that can lead to gum disease, thus promoting healthy gum tissue and reducing swelling. Regular consumption of Mentha x piperita teas can provide relief from gum swelling, promote oral health, and prevent further complications such as bad breath and tooth loss.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh mint leaves (Mentha x piperita) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the mint leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture to remove the mint leaves.
Mix 1 teaspoon of honey (optional) with the mint tea for taste.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help with gum swelling.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Avicenna journal of phytomedicine", Mentha x piperita teas for gum swelling were found to have significant effects on inhibition of oral bacteria biofilm formation.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Mentha x piperita teas can be used to treat gum swelling ailment, but possible side effects include digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with medications such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. Precautions to take when using Mentha x piperita teas include not consuming them in large quantities, especially for people with sensitive stomachs, and being aware of the potential for increased bleeding or lowering of blood sugar levels.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Mentha x piperita tea 3 times a day
Use 1 tablespoon of dried Mentha x piperita leaves per cup
Steep in boiling water for 5-7 minutes
Drink for 7-10 days to reduce gum swelling
Repeat as needed, but consult a doctor if symptoms persist

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm, have been traditionally used to treat the gum swelling ailment known as gingivitis and periodontitis. The properties of Melissa officinalis teas that help to treat this ailment include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce swelling and prevent the growth of bacteria that cause infection. The bioactive constituents of Melissa officinalis teas, such as rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, also contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting wound healing. The benefits of Melissa officinalis teas in treating gum swelling ailments include reduced inflammation, prevention of bacterial growth, and promotion of healthy gum tissue, making it a popular herbal remedy for oral health issues.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers. You can find them in health food stores or online.
Chop the Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers into small pieces. Use 1 tablespoon of chopped herbs for every cup of boiling water.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot. Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat.
Add the chopped Melissa officinalis to the boiling water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup. Discard the herbs and let the tea cool down. Drink 1-2 cups of the tea 3-4 times a day to help with gum swelling.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be effective in treating gum swelling, but using it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions to take when using Melissa officinalis teas to treat gum swelling include avoiding excessive consumption, which may lead to interactions with other medications, and being aware of potential allergic reactions to the tea, especially in people with sensitive stomachs or allergies to certain plants.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Melissa officinalis tea 2-3 times a day.
Take 1-2 teaspoons of dried Melissa officinalis flowers per cup.
Use for 5-7 days for noticeable reduction in gum swelling.
Combine with other treatments as directed by a healthcare professional.
Monitor gum swelling and adjust dosage as recommended.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, also known as licorice root teas, have been used for centuries to treat the gum swelling ailment known as gingivitis and periodontitis. The anti-inflammatory properties of glycyrrhizin, a bioactive constituent found in Glycyrrhiza glabra, help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the gums. This herbal preparation also contains flavonoids and triterpenoids that have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby promoting healing and reducing inflammation in the affected area. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat gum swelling include reduced inflammation, pain relief, and improved oral health, making it a popular natural remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the roots in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the liquid, if desired, to sweeten the tea.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help with gum swelling, but consult a doctor first.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Indian journal of dental research : official publication of Indian Society for Dental Research", Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have shown potential antibacterial activity against primary plaque colonizers and periodontal pathogens.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat gum swelling ailments, but potential side effects include hypertension, edema, and potassium deficiency due to the increased levels of aldosterone, which can be caused by the licorice content in these teas. Precautions to take include monitoring blood pressure and potassium levels, reducing the dose if symptoms occur, and avoiding long-term use to minimize the risk of adverse effects.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 teaspoon of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea in 1 cup of boiling water.
Drink 1 cup of tea 3 times a day for gum swelling relief.
Continue tea consumption for 7-10 days for noticeable results.
Monitor gum swelling and adjust tea consumption as needed.
Consult a healthcare professional if gum swelling persists or worsens.

By consuming oregano tea, individuals can experience relief from gum swelling as the tea helps to reduce pain and discomfort, promoting overall oral health. Regular consumption of oregano tea has been found to be beneficial in treating gum swelling ailments, offering a natural and effective solution for those seeking alternative treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Origanum vulgare leaves or 1/2 cup of dried leaves.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the leaves in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the leaves for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors to release.
Strain the tea into another cup to remove the leaves. Discard the leaves.
Drink the tea hot or let it cool to soothe gum swelling.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the ethnobotanical study, Origanum vulgare teas were cited by herbalists in the north-central region of Morocco as a remedy for oral and gum infections.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Origanum vulgare teas can be effective in treating gum swelling ailments, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, increased heart rate, and interactions with certain medications such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. Precautions should be taken to avoid consuming large quantities, which may cause stomach upset, and to be aware of any sensitivity to the plant's essential oils, which can exacerbate conditions like asthma and high blood pressure.

preparations.herb_desc: Calendula officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat gum swelling ailments, such as gingivitis and periodontitis, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Calendula officinalis, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and carotenoids, help to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth, thereby alleviating gum swelling and promoting healthy gum tissue. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation also help to prevent infection and promote wound healing, making it an effective remedy for treating gum inflammation and swelling. By incorporating Calendula officinalis teas into one's oral care routine, individuals can benefit from its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, promoting a healthy and pain-free mouth.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Calendula officinalis flowers or 2 teaspoons of dried Calendula flowers.
Bring 2 cups of water to a boil in a pot.
Add the gathered Calendula flowers to the boiling water and reduce heat to a simmer.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into a cup.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature, 2-3 times a day, as needed to reduce gum swelling.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Supportive care in cancer : official journal of the Multinational Association of Supportive Care in Cancer, Calendula officinalis teas may be effective in improving the lesions and reducing severity of gum swelling associated with mucositis.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Calendula officinalis teas can cause allergic reactions, such as skin rashes and itching, in some individuals, and may interact with certain medications, including blood thinners and diabetes medications, which can exacerbate gum swelling. To use Calendula officinalis teas safely, take precautions to avoid applying excessive amounts to the affected area, as this can lead to skin irritation and dryness, and also be aware that pregnant or breastfeeding women may want to avoid using this herbal preparation due to its potential effects on fetal development or infant health.

preparations.herb_desc: Camellia sinensis teas, which include various types of green, black, and oolong teas, have been traditionally used to treat gum swelling, also known as gingivitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Camellia sinensis, such as theaflavins and thearubigins, help to reduce swelling and inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines. These herbal preparations also contain catechins, which exhibit antimicrobial properties that help to control the growth of bacteria and fungi that contribute to gum inflammation. The benefits of using Camellia sinensis teas to treat gum swelling include reduced inflammation, improved oral health, and a lower risk of developing gum-related infections.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of Camellia sinensis leaves and 1 cup of water.
Heat the water in a pot until it boils, then turn off the heat.
Add the Camellia sinensis leaves to the hot water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a fine-mesh sieve.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 3 times a day to help with gum swelling.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Camellia sinensis teas for gum swelling may be beneficial in reducing both plaque index (PI) and gingival index (GI), particularly when used in the form of mouthwash, toothpaste, or gel.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Camellia sinensis teas can be used to treat gum swelling, but possible side effects include digestive issues, such as bloating and stomach upset, due to the high caffeine content and other compounds found in the tea. Precautions should be taken when using these teas, including starting with small amounts, avoiding consumption in large quantities, and being mindful of interactions with other medications, such as blood thinners and certain antidepressants.

preparations.herb_desc: Artemisia absinthium teas have been traditionally used to treat gum swelling, also known as gingivitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, including thujone, borneol, and camphor, help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections that cause gum swelling. By using Artemisia absinthium teas, individuals can alleviate symptoms such as redness, swelling, and pain in the gums, promoting a healthier oral environment. The benefits of using these teas include a reduced risk of gum disease progression and a decrease in the need for conventional treatments, making them a popular herbal remedy for gum health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Artemisia absinthium leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the dried leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 3 times a day for gum swelling relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Iranian journal of basic medical sciences, Artemisia absinthium teas have not been mentioned as a treatment for gum swelling in the provided study, as it focuses on gastrointestinal ailments treated with topical remedies in Traditional Persian Medicine.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Artemisia absinthium teas can be used to treat gum swelling, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, stomach upset, and interactions with other medications, particularly those affecting blood pressure and heart rate. Precautions when using these teas include being cautious with the dosage, as excessive consumption may lead to increased heart rate, anxiety, and tremors, and also avoiding concurrent use with other stimulants or medications.

preparation_study: According to Dentistry journal, Echinacea purpurea teas for gum swelling may provide anti-inflammatory actions, which could help alleviate inflammation and swelling associated with gum conditions, such as gingivitis and periodontitis.
